recalls.Description: Leatherman has announced a recall of its Charge Plus and Charge Plus TTi multi-tools due to a potential laceration hazard. The Charge Plus models are available in black and stainless steel, accompanied by either a black MOLLE or nylon sheath. The Charge Plus TTi variant is sold in stainless steel with a black nylon sheath, and all products feature the 'Leatherman' brand name etched on them.
recalls.Title: Leatherman Charge Plus Multi-Tools Recalled Due to Laceration Risk
recalls.ConsumerContact: For assistance, contact Leatherman at 800-847-8665 from 6 a.m. to 5 p.m. PT, Monday through Friday, or via email at charge@leatherman.com. More details can be found online at www.leatherman.com/pages/charge-recall or by visiting www.leatherman.com and selecting 'Safety Recall' at the bottom of the page.

Images.Caption: DRecalled Leatherman Charge Plus TTi (stainless steel)
An exposed blade tip protruding from the handle

Manufacturers.Name: Leatherman Tool Group Inc., of Portland, Oregon

Retailers.Name: Available at REI and various hardware and cutlery stores across the country, as well as online at Leatherman.com, Amazon.com, and REI.com from July 2024 through March 2025, priced between $150 and $200.

Hazards.Name: Laceration hazard due to knife blade not fully retracting

Remedies.Name: Stop using the recalled multi-tools immediately. Visit www.leatherman.com/pages/charge-recall to check if your tool is affected. No proof of purchase is required, and a pre-paid shipping label will be provided for returning the tools for free repairs.

recalls.metaDescription: Leatherman recalls Charge Plus Multi-Tools due to a laceration hazard. Check if your tool is affected and learn how to return it for repair.

recalls.Description: GiantMouse is recalling its GM12 and GMP12 button-lock folding knives due to a potential laceration risk. These knives feature milled scales made of titanium or bronze and are uniquely numbered inside the back spacer. The brand logo and country of origin can be found etched on one of the handle scales.
recalls.Title: GiantMouse Recalls GM12 and GMP12 Folding Knives Due to Laceration Risk
recalls.ConsumerContact: For assistance, consumers can reach out to GiantMouse LLC at 833-665-6777, available 24/7, via email at support@giantmouse.com, or visit www.giantmouse.com/recall. Additional information can be found by clicking on the "Learn More" banner on their website.

Images.Caption: Recalled GiantMouse Folding Knife GM12 (top) Recalled GiantMouse Folding Knife GMP12 (bottom)

Injuries.Name: The firm has received eight reports of the lock failing during use. No injuries have been reported.

Retailers.Name: Available online at www.giantmouse.com from November 14 to November 15, 2024, priced between $385 and $425.

Importers.Name: GiantMouse LLC, of East Lansing, Michigan

Hazards.Name: Risk of laceration due to potential failure of the locking mechanism

Remedies.Name: Stop using the knives immediately and contact GiantMouse for a full refund or free repair. A pre-paid shipping label will be provided for returning the knives, and no proof of purchase is required.

recalls.metaDescription: GiantMouse recalls GM12 and GMP12 folding knives due to a laceration hazard. Contact them for refunds or repairs.
